title: Route npm through cmd.exe on Windows to comply with Node 18.20+ CVE-2024-27980
context: Node >=18.20 refuses to spawn .cmd shim files (like npm) directly without a shell, enforcing CVE-2024-27980 mitigation. This blocks clean-install rehearsal on Windows.
decision: Detect npm commands on Windows and route them through cmd.exe with custom argument quoting (winQuote), replicating the existing pattern from the gate runner.
consequences: npm executions on Windows now route through cmd.exe with manual shell quoting
Cross-platform execution paths diverge (Windows via cmd.exe, Unix direct spawn)
Argument escaping must handle cmd.exe special characters (spaces, quotes, pipes, etc.)
Rehearsal script now mirrors gate runner's Windows shim strategy (referenced dec_812d887be0)
alternatives_rejected: Global shell: true option — too broad, doesn't target npm specifically
Requiring users to upgrade Node or work around it — leaves tooling broken
Using a different package manager — doesn't address the root constraint

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Global shell: true option — too broad, doesn't target npm specifically
Requiring users to upgrade Node or work around it — leaves tooling broken
Using a different package manager — doesn't address the root constraint
